message:
test(workspace): replace let-else+panic with assert+if-let in roundtrip test

In `workspace.rs::tests::*roundtrip*`, the leaf-extension assertion
previously used `let-else { panic!("...") }` to refine the
`PaneNodeKind` enum to its `Leaf(_)` variant. That style gives a
panic message but stops the test on the first variant mismatch and
makes the failure look like an internal panic rather than a test
assertion.

Switch both sites (the encode side and the decode side) to:

    assert!(matches!(&node.kind, PaneNodeKind::Leaf(_)));
    if let PaneNodeKind::Leaf(leaf) = &node.kind { ... }

The `assert!` is the user-facing failure (test runner reports it
as a normal assertion). The `if let` block then runs the leaf
extension check; if the assert above failed, the if-let body is
just skipped (the test will already be marked failed by then).

Behavior identical: same property exercised
(round-tripping `extensions` on both the WorkspaceSnapshot and the
inner Leaf), no change to the production code path. Pure test-
ergonomic cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
